insight:
Yesterday was a solid recovery day with your body showing good signs of readiness. You hit 9,653 steps without any formal training, burned 2,471 calories (which is actually above your 2,300 target), and maintained excellent cardiovascular markers with a resting heart rate of 49 bpm and low stress at 34/100. Your HRV of 95ms sits perfectly within your baseline range of 93-115ms.

With your HRV right in the sweet spot and low stress levels, your nervous system is recovered and ready for today's planned session. Six days out from Chester, this is exactly where you want to be - go ahead with your scheduled workout as planned. Your body is telling you it can handle the training load.

The one concern is that 5.8 hours of sleep - you need at least 7-8 hours this close to race day. Prioritize getting to bed earlier tonight to support your final week of preparation and optimize recovery.
